extension_input_guardrails.root_cause: The Project OS extension capture-phase global slash shortcut intercepted native textarea input because the guard used (!inEditable || !insideExtension).
do_not_repeat: Do not widen document-level slash shortcuts to steal keys from any editable element.
Do not reintroduce insideExtension-based shortcut gating for the native Message Hermes textarea.
Do not change slash interception without keeping native textarea typed '/' and '?' working on phone and laptop.
required_guards: extensions/project-os/project-os-extension.js keeps the global slash shortcut gated by !inEditable.
static/boot.js keeps native composer beforeinput and keydown fallback slash insertion.
extensions/project-os/project-os-extension.js keeps explicit composer slash/question insertion and mobile paste/composition interaction guards.

verification.user_qa: Typed '/' and '?' now work on both phone and laptop; paste also works on both.

change_authority_policy.summary: Automation may apply even larger changes without pausing first when they remain inside the originally agreed Project OS purpose and UX flow.
must_hold: Do not violate the original Project OS purpose.
Do not violate the originally agreed UX flow.
Do not introduce a shadow truth layer or separate goal engine.
When purpose/UX fit becomes ambiguous mid-course, stop broadening and bring that branch back for explicit discussion.
change_authority_policy.planning_rule: Concretize purpose and UX flow early via grilling or a goal-wizard style planning step, then let automation move aggressively inside that envelope.

product_goal.top_level_purpose: Build an automated organization that can run multiple projects/cards broadly in parallel.
product_goal.current_tactic: Governor-first / first-open-gate discipline remains the current execution tactic for the active proof chain.
product_goal.tension_to_manage: Do not let near-term gate discipline collapse into a single-project-only product direction.
